description_long: This object consists of a group of six wooden barrels, each reinforced with dark metal hoops, arranged in two rows of three. The barrels are supported by a sturdy black wooden or metal rack that elevates and organizes them. The wood staves of the barrels are a rich, reddish-brown color, and the metal bands are matte gray, giving the barrels a classic, rustic appearance. The tops and sides of the barrels are visible, showing the circular ends and the segmented construction typical of traditional cooperage. There are no visible handles, spigots, or markings on the barrels, and the rack appears to be designed for stable storage rather than transport. This setup is commonly found in urban environments such as taverns, cellars, or storage areas where bulk liquids or dry goods are kept.
description: A set of six wooden barrels with metal hoops, arranged on a black rack for organized storage. The barrels have a rustic appearance and are unmarked, with no visible handles or lids.
description_view_0: Front view showing all six barrels arranged in two rows of three, with the tops and metal hoops clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the length of the barrels and the supporting rack, with three barrels stacked horizontally and three standing upright.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back ends of the barrels and the full structure of the supporting rack.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horizontal alignment of the barrels on the rack and the upright barrels beside it.
